sessions.intent: Design fix for builder onError dropping and CacheDynamicKey literal rewriting

before.assumptions: Builder LLM inconsistently drops onError properties from .pr files and rewrites intentional literal values

sessions.plan: Strengthen BuildGoal.llm prompt with CRITICAL rules for onError preservation and literal value preservation. Write 6 PLang test suites.

actions.context: Architect plan with prompt changes and 6 test suites

after.health: Plan written, ready for test-designer and coder
after.notes: Test suites may already exist on runtime2 after plang-test-gaps merge

sessions.intent: Assess test coverage for builder onError fix — determine if new tests are needed

before.assumptions: Architect specified 6 PLang test suites. Branch was behind runtime2 which merged runtime2-plang-test-gaps with 33 test suites.
before.risk: Low — assessment only, no code changes

sessions.plan: Merge runtime2 into branch to get existing tests. Compare architect's 6 test specs against existing coverage. Write verdict.

actions.context: All 6 test suites from architect's plan already exist on runtime2. Compared each against architect's spec — full coverage.
Verdict: no new tests needed. Existing suite covers architect's requirements.

after.health: All test coverage verified. No gaps found.
after.notes: Only remaining work is BuildGoal.llm prompt improvement (coder task). No C# tests applicable — fix targets LLM behavior.

sessions.intent: Independent gap analysis of onError PLang test coverage — create 3 new test suites

before.assumptions: v1 only validated architect's list. Need independent analysis of full onError modifier space.
before.risk: Low — creating new test files only

sessions.plan: Map all 6 onError patterns from BuildGoal.llm against existing tests. Identify untested patterns: bare retry, timed retry (ms), GoalFirst order, mixed strategies. Create 3 test suites (11 files).

actions.context: Mapped full onError modifier syntax and runtime types to identify coverage gaps
Bare retry + timed retry (ms) — tests builder generates retryCount and retryOverMs
GoalFirst order — only RetryFirst was previously tested
Mixed strategies (ignore + call + retry+ignore) in one goal — stresses builder

after.health: 3 test suites created (11 files). Full onError modifier space now covered.
after.notes: Design finding: retry time should be ms not seconds. Coder needs to update prompt and consider runtime property rename.

sessions.intent: Code analysis of all changes on runtime2-builder-onerror-fix vs runtime2

before.assumptions: Branch has prompt changes, RetryOverSeconds->RetryOverMs rename, and new test suites. Need to verify rename completeness and test quality.
before.risk: Low — analysis only, no code changes

sessions.plan: Review all 61 changed files across 5 analysis passes: OBP compliance, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning (trace retryOverSeconds->retryOverMs rename end-to-end), deletion test (do PLang tests actually verify retry behavior?).

actions.context: FAIL: Stale .pr file still has retryOverSeconds in JSON schema at line 181. Builder will tell LLM to use wrong field name.
NEEDS WORK: Tests verify error propagation but not retry count, timing, or ordering. Would pass even if retry was completely broken.

after.health: Verdict: FAIL. Stale .pr blocks correct builder operation. Test quality gap in retry tests.
after.notes: Coder must rebuild system/builder/ to fix stale .pr. Test-designer should consider strengthening retry tests to verify actual retry behavior.

sessions.intent: Fix stale builder .pr and strengthen retry PLang tests per codeanalyzer findings

before.assumptions: Builder .pr has retryOverSeconds but source already has retryOverMs. Retry tests only check error propagation, not actual retry counts.
before.risk: Low for .pr rename. Medium for test restructuring — changed retry ownership from throw step to call step.

sessions.plan: 1. Replace retryOverSeconds with retryOverMs in builder .pr files. 2. Restructure ErrorRetryOnly and ErrorGoalFirst tests: add counter variables in called goals, move retry from throw to call step, assert counter values. 3. Delete stale .build folders. 4. Note blocker: no OpenAI API key for rebuilding.

actions.context: Mechanical rename retryOverSeconds -> retryOverMs to align .pr with source .goal/.llm. Manual edit because no LLM API key available to rebuild.
Restructured: added counter increments in called goals, moved retry from throw to call step, added counter assertions
Deleted stale .build folders — goal files changed, need LLM rebuild

after.health: Code changes done. Tests need LLM rebuild (no OpenAI API key in env).
after.notes: Run 'plang p build --llmservice=openai' in ErrorRetryOnly/ and ErrorGoalFirst/ when API key is available, then run 'plang p !test' to verify.

sessions.intent: Revert manual .pr edits and rebuild properly with plang builder

before.assumptions: v1 manually edited .pr files which is never allowed. OpenAI API key is now available.
before.risk: Low — reverting to builder-generated output and rebuilding

sessions.plan: 1. Revert manual .pr edits via git checkout HEAD~2. 2. Rebuild system/builder with plang builder (force rebuild by deleting stale BuildGoal folder). 3. Rebuild ErrorRetryOnly and ErrorGoalFirst test .pr files. 4. Verify retryOverMs in rebuilt files.

actions.context: Reverted manual edits then deleted stale per-step folder to force v0.2 rebuild
Builder regenerated into v0.2 single-file format. No retryOverSeconds anywhere.
Rebuilt test .pr files. ErrorRetryOnly has retryOverMs: 500 correctly.

after.health: All .pr files rebuilt by plang builder. No manual edits. retryOverMs used correctly.
after.notes: Builder now uses v0.2 single-file format for BuildGoal. Old per-step folder deleted.
